tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal impairment or early kidney disease. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no nitrites or leukocytes are detected, making infection less likely. Low HbA1c and normal glucose exclude diabetes. Recommend further renal evaluation and monitoring, and clinical correlation for possible underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
